items.headline: Four downtown businesses permitted to update digital signs
items.summary: Planning approvals issued for matrix sign graphic modifications at 520 Webster St, 250 Hamilton Ave, 528 High St, and 445 Bryant St. These changes affect signage for multiple downtown commercial tenants.

items.headline: Equinix data center permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center facility receives its building permit at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A expansion permitted on Blossom Hill Road
items.summary: A $809,557 demolition and addition project for Chick-fil-A at 1162 Blossom Hill Road is cleared to build. The work will expand the existing restaurant location.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument agreement finalized for Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: San José City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo, at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument is now formally authorized for installation and maintenance at this downtown riverfront location.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District 2026-2027 budget approved
items.summary: Council took action on the annual assessment and budget report for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District for FY 2026-27. Business improvement districts fund enhanced maintenance, security, and marketing in their designated areas through property owner assessments.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed an update to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. The General Plan guides zoning, development, and city services; updates to this element shape how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager on May 19, 2026.

items.headline: City to present draft water shortage contingency plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The contingency plan outlines how the city would respond if water supplies fall short, which affects residents' access to water and any potential restrictions during drought conditions.
